item.description: - Fixed an issue where typed text could be invisible in notes on some Macs. - Added a translucent (glass) note style — choose Solid or Glass in Settings. - Redesigned the App Updates tab and made release notes easier to read. - CueNow now checks for updates automatically and shows what's new. - Added numbered lists; type "1." or "1)" to start one. - Create checklists by typing "- " (hyphen and space). - Improved checklist editing: cleaner deletes and automatic renumbering. - Apply bold, italic, or underline before you start typing. - Added an option to hide CueNow's Dock icon and run from the menu bar.
- Note History now shows more archived notes at once and scrolls on its own. - You can now hide CueNow's Dock icon and run it from the menu bar.

- Saves the text you're actively typing in a note before quitting, archiving, deleting, or running an in-app update — so your latest words are never left behind. - The Support item in the menubar dropdown now opens Settings directly on the Support tab. - Improved note storage path handling for broader macOS version compatibility, and made pre-update backup failures non-blocking so a backup hiccup can't stop your notes from being saved.
